zh.lifeSummary: 當Aelfwine 'Ailwin' Alywyne Horne Of Herefordshire, Sheriff of Warwick於大約1042年出生在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾馬爾斯頓 格林時，他的父親Wigot Warwick43歲，母親Ermenhilde de Mercia38歲。他和Cecilia至少有4個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於大約1083年在英國瓦維其可爾享年43歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Aelfwine 'Ailwin' Alywyne Horne Of Herefordshire, Sheriff of Warwick于大约1042年出生在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾馬爾斯頓 格林时，他的父亲Wigot Warwick43岁，母亲Ermenhilde de Mercia38岁。他和Cecilia至少有4个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于大约1083年在英国瓦維其可爾享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: Warwick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Warwick, the county seat of Warwickshire, or from the county itself. The placename derives from Old English wering, wæring ‘dam’ (a derivative of wer, wær ‘weir’) + wīc ‘dwelling, specialized farmstead’ (dative plural wīcum).
habitational name from a much smaller place of the same name in Cumbria. This placename probably derives from Old English waroth ‘bank, shore’ + wīc.

variants.name: Warrick
meanings: from Middle English warrok ‘wedge or twisting stick used to tighten the lashings on a scaffold’, perhaps a metonymic occupational name for a maker of warrocks or a builder of scaffolds. In the building trades the lashings for scaffolds were tightened by driving in wedges called warrocks.

variants.name: Worrick
meanings: English: variant of   Warwick  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: 'Ailwin'
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Alvin
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ælf ‘elf, supernatural being’ + wine ‘friend’. This was not especially common in Britain either before or after the Norman Conquest, but it is popular in the United States. The reasons for this are not entirely clear; association with Calvin may be a factor. A more plausible (though less elevated) explanation is that this was the name given to the naughty chipmunk in a popular television cartoon series of the 1960s.

nameMeanings.name: Horne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mainly Canadian: of uncertain derivation, but most probably taken from the name of the territory of Lorn(e) in Argyll (and thus a masculine form of Lorna ). One of the earliest bearers was the Canadian actor Lorne Greene ( 1915–1987 ), and the given name is now also found in Scotland.

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Sheriff
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name or nickname from Middle English shirreve, sher(r)eve, sheref(fe) ‘sheriff, officer of the Crown’ (Old English scīrgerēfa, from scīr ‘shire, county’ + (ge)rēfa ‘reeve’), literally the reeve (i.e. steward) of a shire. Compare Reeve .
Muslim (mainly Nigeria, Sierra Leone, and Liberia): variant of   Sharif  . In Sierra Leone and Liberia this name is found mainly among the Mandinka people. Compare   Sirleaf  .

variants.name: Sirleaf
meanings: West African (Liberia): altered form of the predominantly Mandinka Muslim name   Sheriff  .

variants.name: Reeve
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Rhys . The name is now in widespread and frequent use outside Wales, in many cases representing a transferred use of the surname so spelled, which is derived from the given name.

variants.name: Shreve
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Sheriff  . Compare   Shreeve  ,   Shreves  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

en.lifeSummary: When Aelfwine 'Ailwin' Alywyne Horne Of Herefordshire, Sheriff of Warwick was born about 1042, in Marston Green, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Wigot Warwick, was 43 and his mother, Ermenhilde de Mercia, was 38. He had at least 4 sons and 1 daughter with Cecilia. He died about 1083, in Warwickshire, England, at the age of 43.

Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille avec Cecilia. Il est décédé environ 1083, à Warwickshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 43 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Aelfwine 'Ailwin' Alywyne Horne Of Herefordshire, Sheriff of Warwick(이)가 대략 1042년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 마스턴 그린에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Wigot Warwick(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Ermenhilde de Mercia(은)는 38세였다. 그는 Cecilia(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1083년 영국 워윅실르에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.
